Forbearance and Restructuring for Farm Loans, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–387, title III, § 313(a)102 Stat. 949Aug. 11, 1988“It is the sense of Congress that the Secretary of Agriculture should, with respect to farmers and ranchers who suffer major losses due to drought, hail, excessive moisture, or related condition in 1988—“(1)exercise forbearance in the collection of interest and principal on direct farmer program loans under the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act [ et seq.] outstanding for such farmers and ranchers;7 U.S.C. 1921“(2)expedite the use of credit restructuring and other credit relief mechanisms authorized under the Agricultural Credit Act of 1987 [, , , see Tables for classification] and similar provisions of law for such farmers and ranchers; andPub. L. 100–233101 Stat. 1568Jan. 6, 1988“(3)encourage commercial lenders participating in guaranteed farmer lending programs under the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to exercise forbearance before declaring loans to such farmers and ranchers under such programs in default.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: In addition to any other authority that the Secretary may have to defer principal and interest and forego foreclosure, the Secretary may permit, at the request of the borrower, the deferral of principal and interest on any outstanding loan made, insured, or held by the Secretary under this chapter, or under the provisions of any other law administered by the Farmers Home Administration or by the Rural Development Administration, and may forego foreclosure of any such loan, for such period as the Secretary deems necessary upon a showing by the borrower that due to circumstances beyond the borrower’s control, the borrower is temporarily unable to continue making payments of such principal and interest when due without unduly impairing the standard of living of the borrower. The Secretary may permit interest that accrues during the deferral period on any loan deferred under this section to bear no interest during or after such period: , That if the security instrument securing such loan is foreclosed such interest as is included in the purchase price at such foreclosure shall become part of the principal and draw interest from the date of foreclosure at the rate prescribed by law.Provided

children.label: A
children.text: has pending against the Department a claim of program discrimination that is accepted by the Department as valid; or

children.label: B
children.text: files a claim of program discrimination that is accepted by the Department as valid.

children.heading: Waiver of interest and offsets
children.label: 2 Waiver of interest and offsets
children.text: During the period of the moratorium, the Secretary shall waive the accrual of interest and offsets on all farmer program loans made under subchapter I, II, or III for which loan acceleration or foreclosure proceedings have been suspended under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: the date the Secretary resolves the claim; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the farmer or rancher appeals the decision of the Secretary on the claim to a court of competent jurisdiction, the date that the court renders a final decision on the claim.

children.heading: Failure to prevail
children.label: 4 Failure to prevail
children.text: If a farmer or rancher does not prevail on a claim of discrimination described in paragraph (1), the farmer or rancher shall be liable for any interest and offsets that accrued during the period that loan acceleration or foreclosure proceedings have been suspended under paragraph (1).
